FAQ

Questions, answered clearly.

Start here before you apply, foster, volunteer, or donate. If you still have questions, reach out.

Apply to adopt Contact

Helpful basics

Clear answers help dogs get home.

What kind of dogs does Underdog Heroes focus on?

Dogs who have been abused, neglected, overlooked, or need extra care before they can safely move into a home. Some are large breeds, and some are simply dogs who need time, structure, and the right people.

Why is the adoption process so serious?

Because the right match protects everyone. The application helps us understand your home, your routine, and which dog can truly thrive with you.

What should adopters stop assuming?

Please do not assume a rescued dog is ready for a picture-perfect first week. New home, new people, new sounds, and new routines are a lot. Most dogs need decompression, patience, and consistency.

Does fostering cost money?

Fostering is free. The rescue provides supplies and financial support until the foster dog is adopted.

Why monthly donations?

One-time gifts help immediately, and monthly donors create stability. Steady support means the rescue can say yes before the next emergency goes viral.

What happens when a dog is adopted?

We give the dog a couple of weeks in the adoptive home before celebrating. Then the profile can become a happy ending.

Can first-time large-dog or rescue-dog owners apply?

Yes, depending on the dog. Some dogs need prior experience, a specific home setup, no young kids, no other pets, or a commitment to training. We will talk through fit honestly.

How can local people help if they cannot adopt?

Foster, volunteer, transport, help with care work, share specific needs, donate, or become a monthly donor. It takes community.

Underdog Heroes

Dog rescue for abused, neglected, and overlooked dogs who deserve a fighting chance.

Adopt: Adopt@underdogheroes.org Foster: Foster@underdogheroes.org Volunteer: info@underdogheroes.org Instagram
